originalTitle: Police fire shots in air to disperse angry crowds at DR Congo Ebola treatment centre
title: Our Friends in DR Congo: Staying Safe from Sickness
content:
Hello from Your Africa News Friend!

Hello, super kids! It's me, your friendly news reporter, here to tell you something important happening in our amazing continent, Africa. We are talking about our friends in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. It's a big country with lots of beautiful places and lovely people, just like where you live! Sometimes, things happen that make people worried or sad. My job is to explain these things in a way that helps you understand, and remember, we are all one big family in Africa.

What is Happening in DR Congo?

Imagine you have a friend who is very sick. Doctors try their best to help them get better. Sadly, sometimes people get sick with a very bad germ called Ebola. In DR Congo, some people have been getting this sickness. When someone gets sick with Ebola, they need special care. Sometimes, very sadly, people die from this sickness. This makes their families very, very sad. When someone dies from Ebola, it's very important to bury them in a special way. This special way helps stop the sickness from spreading to other people. But sometimes, families want to bury their loved ones in their usual way, because that is their custom. This makes them feel sad and confused. They want to bring their family members home. Because of this, sometimes many people gather. They feel very, very sad and worried. The doctors and nurses, and even the police, are there to help everyone stay safe. Sometimes, to help everyone remember to keep a safe distance and to keep order, the police might make a loud noise. They do this so that everyone knows it's important to move back a little bit and stay safe. It's not to hurt anyone, but to make sure the sickness doesn't spread more.

What is Ebola?

Let's talk a little bit more about Ebola. What is it? Ebola is a type of sickness, like when you get a bad flu or a cold, but it's much, much stronger. It is caused by very tiny germs, so tiny you can't see them without a special microscope! These tiny germs can make people very, very sick. People with Ebola can get a fever, feel very tired, and have stomach aches. It's like having the worst tummy bug ever, but much more dangerous. Doctors and nurses work very hard to help sick people feel better and try to fight off the germs. **Did you know?** The first time doctors found Ebola was in a village near the Ebola River in Africa. That's how it got its name! It's a special kind of germ that needs special care from clever doctors. Ebola germs love to spread. They can spread from one person to another if you touch someone who is sick, or if you touch their clothes, or even if you touch things they have touched. This is why doctors and nurses wear special suits, gloves, and masks. They look a bit like astronauts! They wear these suits to protect themselves from the tiny germs and to keep everyone safe.

Why Do We Need Special Care for Loved Ones Who Passed Away?

This is a very important part, but it's a bit sad. When someone dies from Ebola, even after they have passed away, the tiny Ebola germs can still be inside their body. These germs can still spread to other people if they touch the body. This is why it's super important to bury people who died from Ebola in a very, very special and safe way. Imagine you have a magic bubble around the germs. Doctors and special burial teams put the person's body in a special, sealed bag. They then help to bury them safely and with respect. This stops the tiny germs from jumping out and making other people sick. It's like putting a lid on a pot to keep the steam inside. It's a very difficult thing for families, because usually, families want to wash their loved ones or hold them one last time. They want to follow their family traditions, which are very important. But with Ebola, doing this can make many more people sick. The helpers, like doctors and special burial teams, explain this to the families. They know it's hard and that families are hurting. They try to do everything with great respect for the person and their family. Their main job is to keep everyone in the village or town safe and healthy, so that no more people get sick from this tricky germ.

Who Are the Helpers?

There are many brave helpers in DR Congo! There are doctors and nurses who work tirelessly in the treatment centres. These are special hospitals just for people with Ebola. They give medicine and comfort to those who are sick. They wear their special suits every day. They are like superheroes without capes! There are also community workers. These are people from the local villages and towns who talk to their neighbors. They explain what Ebola is, how it spreads, and why it's important to follow safety rules. They are very important because they help people understand in a kind way and help build trust. And yes, there are also police officers. Their job is to keep everyone safe and to make sure things don't get too crowded or messy. Sometimes, when people are very upset, they might make noise or try to go to places that are not safe. The police are there to make sure everyone keeps a safe distance from the germs and from each other, especially near a place where sick people are being cared for. They want to keep everyone calm and safe, like a crossing guard helps kids cross the street safely. **Did you know?** Many countries around the world send doctors and nurses to help fight Ebola in Africa. People from all over care about our health and safety and want to help our continent!

Why Are People Sad and Worried?

It's very easy to understand why people in DR Congo are sad and worried. First, losing a family member is always very, very sad. It makes your heart ache a lot. It's like losing your favorite toy, but much, much worse, because it's a person you love. Second, not being able to say goodbye in the usual way, with traditional ceremonies, can make people feel even sadder and confused. Imagine if you couldn't hug your mummy or daddy goodbye, or if you couldn't do the special things your family always does when someone passes away. That would be very hard. People also worry about getting sick themselves. They worry about their children and their friends. This worry can make people feel scared and sometimes angry. They might not always understand why the doctors and police are doing what they do. They might think the doctors are taking their loved ones away, instead of trying to protect everyone. This is why the community workers are so important – to explain everything in a kind and clear way, helping people to trust the helpers and understand the big picture.

What Is Being Done to Help?

Lots of good people are working very hard to help in DR Congo. They are doing many things to keep everyone safe and healthy: 1. **Teaching about Ebola**: They are teaching everyone about how Ebola spreads and how to keep safe. They teach people to wash their hands often, not to touch sick people without protection, and to go to the doctor quickly if they feel unwell. It's like learning the rules of a game so you can play safely. 2. **Safe burials**: They are showing families how to have respectful and safe burials. They explain that these special burials protect the living, so no more people get sick. They try to make these burials as caring as possible. 3. **Vaccines**: Doctors also have special medicine called a vaccine. It's like a shield that can help protect people from getting Ebola! They are giving this vaccine to people who are at risk, like doctors, nurses, and family members of sick people. These vaccines help keep communities healthy. 4. **Building trust**: Most importantly, everyone is working to build trust. They want people to know that the doctors, nurses, and police are all there to help them, not to make things harder. They are friends, not enemies, and they want to help everyone live a long, healthy life. **Did you know?** Handwashing with soap and water is one of the best ways to stop many germs from spreading, not just Ebola! It's like magic for your hands, washing all the tiny baddies away!

A Message of Hope and Safety

So, what should we remember? We remember that there are brave helpers working every day in DR Congo to keep everyone safe from Ebola. We remember that it's okay to feel sad and worried, but it's important to listen to the people who are trying to help. We remember that special care, like safe burials, is done to protect *everyone* in the community from getting sick. It's about keeping all our friends and family healthy. Africa is a continent of strong, kind people. By working together, listening to our helpers, and understanding how to stay safe, we can help our friends in DR Congo get through this. Sending big hugs to all our friends there! Stay safe, everyone!
summary: In DR Congo, doctors, nurses, and police are working hard to stop the spread of a sickness called Ebola, even when it means explaining difficult rules about safe burials to families who are very sad.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how people work together to keep everyone healthy and safe from sickness, especially in times of worry, and how we learn to trust helpers who are trying to protect us all.

originalTitle: Kenya police shake up president's protection team after security breach
title: Big News: Keeping Our President Safe in Kenya!
content:
Hello, super friends! It's your friendly news reporter from Africa, here to tell you something important. Today, we have special news from a beautiful country called Kenya. It's all about making sure our leaders are always safe and sound!

Who is President Ruto?

Every country has a very important leader. In Kenya, that leader is President William Ruto. He is like the head teacher for the whole country! President Ruto works very hard for all the people in Kenya. He helps make sure schools are good and everyone can be healthy. He wants to make Kenya a wonderful place for kids like you. Did you know? A president is chosen by the people to lead them! They listen to what people need and try to make good changes.

What Happened?

One sunny day, President Ruto was out meeting people. He likes to say hello and listen to what everyone has to say. He was shaking hands and smiling. Suddenly, a man came very close to him. The man hugged the President quickly. It all happened very, very fast. This was not supposed to happen because of important safety rules. Safety rules are like the rules we have at school to keep us safe. They help keep important people from getting too close to strangers without permission. Soon, brave and helpful police officers came quickly. They gently stopped the man and took him away. Everyone made sure President Ruto was okay, and he was safe.

What is "Security"?

You know how grown-ups keep you safe at school or at home? Well, important leaders like presidents also have special people who keep them safe. These special people are called 'security' or 'bodyguards'. They are very strong and very smart police officers. They wear uniforms and watch carefully. Their job is to make sure nobody gets too close to the president without permission. They watch out for anything that might hurt the president or make him worried. Did you know? Police officers train for a very long time to learn how to keep people safe! They practice being brave and quick.

Why is Security Important for a President?

Imagine if your teacher was always safe and could do their job well. It's the same for a president! If the president is safe, they can focus on making good plans for the country. They can visit different towns and meet many people without worry. This helps them understand what everyone needs. Good security means the president can keep working hard for you and me. It helps everyone in the country feel calm and secure, knowing their leader is protected.

What Did the Police Do After?

After the man hugged President Ruto, everyone thought even more about safety. The big bosses of the police wanted to make things extra, extra safe for the president. They decided to make some important changes. They changed some of the police officers who protect the president every day. It's like when a football coach changes players to make the team stronger and better at playing. They called this a 'shake-up' or 'changing the team'. It means they wanted to find the very best and smartest officers for this super important job of protecting the president. Did you know? Police officers wear special uniforms to show they are helpers and protectors in our community!

Making Things Safer!

The new police officers who will protect President Ruto are very good at their job. They will learn even more ways to keep him safe wherever he goes. They will make sure no one can get too close again without permission. This helps everyone feel better and more secure. It shows that safety is super important for our leaders. The police work hard every single day to protect everyone in the country, from the president to children like you.

Did You Know? Fun Facts About Kenya!

Kenya is a beautiful country in Africa! Its capital city is Nairobi, which is a big, busy place with tall buildings and many people. Did you know? Kenya has amazing animals like mighty lions, huge elephants, and tall giraffes! They live in big national parks where they are safe and looked after. The people of Kenya speak two main languages: Swahili and English. "Jambo!" means "Hello!" in Swahili. Can you say Jambo? Kenya is also famous for its very fast runners! Many of the best runners in the world come from Kenya.

Keeping Everyone Safe

So, what did we learn today, my super friends? We learned that President Ruto is safe and sound. We learned that the police are working very hard to protect him. They even changed some teams to make his security even better than before. It's all about keeping our leaders safe so they can help us all. Let's say a big thank you to all the police and security people! They help make our world a safer place for everyone, every single day.
summary: Police in Kenya are changing the team that protects President William Ruto to make sure he is extra safe after a man got too close to him.
whyMatters: It is important because keeping our president safe means he can do his important job to help all the people in the country and make good plans for everyone.

originalTitle: Russia threatens more Kyiv strikes and tells foreign nationals to leave
title: Big News from Far Away: Our Friends in Ukraine
content:
Hello from Your News Friend!

Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your news reporter from Africa here. I have some news to share with you today. It's about a place very, very far away from us. It's important to know what happens in the world, even far away. We will talk about it simply, so everyone can understand what is happening.

A Place Called Kyiv

Imagine a big city, like some of the big cities we have here in Africa. This city is called Kyiv. Can you say "Kee-yiv"? It's the capital city of a country called Ukraine. Ukraine is a country in Europe. It's very far from us. Capital cities are super important! They are like the main home for a country's government, where big decisions are made. Did you know? Kyiv is one of the oldest cities in Europe! It has many beautiful buildings and parks. People there love to play and learn, just like us. It's a busy place with lots of kind people.

Loud Noises in the Sky

Now, something sad happened in Kyiv. On a recent night, there were many loud noises in the sky. Imagine big, big fireworks, but not the fun kind. These were loud booms that sounded scary. They were called "aerial assaults." This means things flew in the sky and made big bangs. These bangs can break things and make people very, very afraid. This happened on a Saturday night. It was one of the biggest times this kind of thing has happened in the country. People just want to sleep safely in their beds. It's not nice when people can't feel safe at home.

What Russia Said Next

After these loud noises, another country called Russia said something. Russia is Ukraine's neighbor. It is a very, very big country. Russia said it might make more loud noises in Kyiv. This is called a "threat." A threat means saying you might do something not nice. Russia said it might send more things that make loud bangs. This makes people worry even more. Nobody likes to hear threats, especially when they mean things could get scary. Did you know? Russia is the largest country in the world! It is so big, it covers two continents! Imagine walking from one side to the other – it would take a very, very long time!

People Are Told to Go Home

Because of these scary things, people from other countries who were living in Kyiv were told something important. They were told to leave Ukraine and go back to their own homes. These people are called "foreign nationals." It means they are not from Ukraine, but they were living or working there. They were told to leave because it might not be safe for them anymore. Imagine if you were visiting a friend in another village, and suddenly it wasn't safe. Your parents would want you to come home quickly, wouldn't they? It's like that. It's always best to be where you are safe.

Why Are Countries Fighting?

Sometimes, countries have big disagreements. When these disagreements become very, very big, and they can't talk them out, they sometimes fight. This is called a "war." War is very sad. It makes people unhappy, it breaks homes, and it stops children from going to school. It can make everyone feel scared. We always hope that countries can be friends and talk about their problems, just like we learn to talk to our friends when we disagree. It is much better to be kind to each other and find peaceful ways to solve problems. Did you know? Ukraine is famous for its beautiful sunflower fields! Sunflowers are very tall and yellow flowers. They always face the sun and are used to make cooking oil!

Our Wish for Peace

Here in Africa, we love peace. We want our homes and villages to be safe and happy. We also want that for children and families all around the world, even in Kyiv. It makes us sad to hear about loud noises and people being scared. We hope that soon, all the fighting will stop. We hope that everyone in Ukraine, and everywhere else, can live safely and happily again. We wish for peace for everyone! We believe that talking and being kind is much stronger than fighting.

Let's Learn More!

Did you know? A capital city is usually where the most important buildings are, like where the president works or where important laws are made. Did you know? When countries are neighbors, it's like living next door to someone. We hope neighbors can always be good friends and help each other. Did you know? Many people in Ukraine speak Ukrainian, which is a different language from English! They also have many delicious foods, like a special soup called borscht. Did you know? The flag of Ukraine is blue and yellow, like the sky above the sunflower fields! It's a beautiful flag for a beautiful country.
summary: Russia warned of more loud bangs in Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, after scary noises already happened, and people from other places are being asked to leave for safety.
whyMatters: It is important because people in Ukraine are feeling scared, and we all hope for peace and safety for children and families everywhere, just like we want peace in our own homes.

originalTitle: Man Utd captain Fernandes takes on Keane over 'lie'
title: Man Utd captain Fernandes takes on Keane over 'lie'
content: Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes accuses Roy Keane of putting "words in my mouth" and says he has asked for the phone number of the club legend to set him straight.

content: Basketball legend Michael Jordan leads the cast of star names to pay tribute to Pep Guardiola at the Manchester City manager's farewell party.

originalTitle: Shaw signs new Man City deal after contract U-turn
title: Shaw signs new Man City deal after contract U-turn
content: Man City striker Khadija Shaw signs a new four-year contract with the club, just days after it looked like she would be leaving the WSL champions this summer.
